evals.prompt: I need to add tests to the gpu-fan-controller project. It's a Python script with no tests at all.
evals.expected_output: Detects greenfield mode, brainstorms testing strategy, uses context7 for pytest docs, generates test infrastructure and test files for gpu_fan_controller.py, runs tests, creates PR

assertions.text: A pytest config file exists (pyproject.toml with pytest section, or pytest.ini, or conftest.py)
At least one test file matching test_*.py or *_test.py was generated
Tests import or reference gpu_fan_controller module
Test files contain actual assert statements, not just pass or empty functions
A testing strategy document was created (any markdown file describing the test plan)
Context7 MCP was invoked to retrieve pytest documentation

evals.prompt: Add tests for the install.sh script in gpu-fan-controller. It's a bash installer script that checks dependencies, loads kernel modules, and copies files.
evals.expected_output: Detects shell/bash language, selects bats-core framework, generates targeted tests for install.sh covering dependency checks, module loading, file copying, and error paths

assertions.text: At least one .bats test file exists in the outputs
A test helper file exists with setup/teardown functions
Tests cover dependency checking logic (e.g., testing what happens when a dependency is missing)
Tests mock or stub external commands (modprobe, cp, systemctl, etc.) rather than running them for real

evals.prompt: Review the test coverage on my project — it has some pytest tests but nothing for the shell scripts or the systemd service integration.
evals.expected_output: Detects coverage-gap mode with multi-language (Python + Shell), audits existing pytest tests, identifies missing shell script tests, generates bats tests and integration tests, presents coverage report

assertions.text: A coverage gap analysis document or report exists identifying what's missing
Bats tests (.bats files) were created for shell scripts
Tests for systemd service integration exist (testing .service file, ExecStart, ExecStop, etc.)
A top-level test runner (Makefile target, run_tests.sh, or equivalent) that runs all language test suites
Output explicitly identifies what areas are missing from the existing pytest tests
The run correctly identified coverage-gap mode rather than greenfield mode
